objective: Generate complete, execution-ready test cases from scenario blueprints with the required classifications and preserved lineage/context. Do not prioritize; prioritization is handled by a separate agent.
rules: Generate full test cases including title, preconditions, test data, domain-specific validations, steps, and expected result.
Ensure each test case maps clearly to one scenario blueprint.
Assign exactly one test case layer: UI, API, Database, ETL Integration, or E2E.
Assign exactly one scenario type: Positive, Negative, Edge Case, or Exception Handling.
Assign functional_test_type only when the test is functional in nature; otherwise keep it empty.
Assign non_functional_type only when the test is non-functional in nature; otherwise keep it empty.
Expected result must validate the business outcome, not only the technical response.
Use domain-specific terminology, validations, and compliance expectations when the domain is known.
Use parameterization where applicable, such as {{baseUrl}}, {{authToken}}, {{entityId}}, {{batchDate}}, {{sourceTable}}, {{targetTable}}.
Avoid duplicate or near-duplicate test cases.
Set automation_hint_initial to Yes when clearly automatable, No when clearly manual, and Maybe when automation is possible but depends on tooling, environment, or setup complexity.
Preserve story_id, story_title, ac_id, ac_text, project_state, application_or_product, module, submodule, scenario_id, base_seed, scenario_family, derived_from, and requirement_gaps from A4.
Keep exactly one test_case_layer and exactly one normalized scenario_type, but do not drop richer lineage fields from A4.
Use a5_scenario_type_hint from A4 where available and map boundary to Edge Case.
